actionable_feedback: Add a machine-readable export of workbook formulas (e.g., a 'Formulas' sheet listing cell addresses and formulas or a CSV of formula cells) so graders can validate executable logic without openpyxl.

Provide the full, untruncated decision memo (or an appended 'Conclusions & Cost Breakdown' section) that includes: explicit throttle_flow_days assumption used, per-action cost arithmetic, and the final chosen action with cumulative cost vs budget.

Include a small Validation excerpt (text or CSV) showing packet_row_id | packet_value | workbook_value | match_flag for a sample of rows to demonstrate that Packet_Data was copied/validated programmatically.
In the workbook, add an explicit 'packet_row_id' column on vessel rows and visible 'needs_review' flags for assumption-driven cells (replace_*/lead_lag_swap assumptions).
Document the score aggregation method used by the grader (or include a 'Score_Audit' note) if a combined semantic score is reported, to make aggregation auditable.
In the trace or a short README, note the fallback spreadsheet generation method (pure-stdlib XML writer) and confirm that formulas are stored as formula XML nodes (not precomputed values).

feedback_summary: Baseline successfully produced the requested workbook and decision memo and the grader/verifier accepted the deliverables. However programmatic verification was limited because preview tooling could not parse the XLSX (openpyxl unavailable) and the memo preview is truncated. The grader/verifier flagged three verification gaps that reduce auditability: (1) inability to programmatically inspect formulas in the workbook, (2) missing explicit cost-duration (throttle_flow_days) and a full cost breakdown in the memo, and (3) truncated memo preventing inspection of final action arithmetic and rationale. Addressing these will make the deliverable verifiable and reduce grader uncertainty.

limitations: Preview tooling could not parse XLSX (openpyxl missing) so grader relied on memo and trace.
Memo preview truncated; final decision/cost arithmetic not fully visible.

Untruncate and supply the full decision memo including cost arithmetic and explicit throttle_flow_days.
Include a Validation excerpt to show packet/workbook value matching.
Document score-aggregation method if final_score is reported from rubric items.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce 611-revised that includes: (1) an added 'Formulas' sheet or CSV export listing all formula cells (address and formula), (2) the full untruncated treatment_decision_memo.md with an explicit cost table and the throttle_flow_days assumption, (3) a Validation excerpt file demonstrating packet-to-workbook value matching, (4) workbook edits to add packet_row_id and needs_review flags, and (5) a short note in the trace/README describing the XML-based workbook generation and confirming formulas are present.
trace_specific_comments: Trace documents a fallback workbook generation (zip/xml) and claims formulas were written into the workbook XML. For auditability, add a short README in the attempt directory describing the XML writer approach and confirming formulas are present (not precomputed values).

Trace step showing openpyxl was unavailable is useful; mention in README whether the produced workbook was validated by opening in Excel or another viewer during generation to ensure formula preservation.
